symptoms.symptom_name: Percutaneous puncture wound to the plantar foot from a discarded needle

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: community needlestick injury with blood-borne virus exposure risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The percutaneous injury from a discarded sharp is the exposure event itself.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unknown source/infection status of the discarded sharp

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: community needlestick injury with blood-borne virus exposure risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Unknown source status is what mandates a formal in-person PEP-indication assessment and baseline serology, while remaining low absolute risk for a dried community needle.

symptoms.symptom_name: Immediate first aid — washed wound with soap and water

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: community needlestick injury with blood-borne virus exposure risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Prompt washing is appropriate first aid that modestly reduces contamination; it does not remove the need for in-person assessment.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tetanus-containing vaccination last given more than 10 years ago

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Contaminated puncture plus overdue booster meets criteria for tetanus prophylaxis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute anxiety, hyperventilation and psychomotor agitation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The dominant presenting feature; drives the patient's request but is not itself a physiological emergency.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: community needlestick injury with blood-borne virus exposure risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Anxiety is a reaction to the exposure fear, not a marker of transmission risk — it must not drive the triage tier upward.

symptoms.symptom_name: Tachycardia (HR 115), hypertension (BP 145/90) and tachypnoea (RR 22) on clinician measurement

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Sympathetic activation consistent with acute anxiety; afebrile and well-perfused, so not an infective/septic pattern. Sealed clinician measurement — the AI Doctor infers distress from the patient's self-report, not a measured vital.

symptoms.symptom_name: Wound depth ~3mm subcutaneous, 1mm entry, minimal surrounding erythema, no active bleeding, needle tip intact (no retained foreign body)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: retained foreign body in the wound
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: In-person inspection with the intact needle available excludes a retained fragment — a clinician-only assessment, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of spreading erythema, red streaks, fever or rigors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No current features of local or systemic infection; these are the safety-net triggers to watch for.

symptoms.symptom_name: Baseline blood-borne virus serology (HIV Ag/Ab, HBsAg/HBsAb, HCV Ab)

investigation_details.expected_result: Establishes the patient's pre-exposure baseline status; results pending and unavailable in real time via telehealth.
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: No baseline against which to interpret any future seroconversion.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: community needlestick injury with blood-borne virus exposure risk
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Baseline serology is standard post-exposure care; venepuncture cannot be performed via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline serology — HIV Ag/Ab, hepatitis B surface antigen and antibody, hepatitis C antibody
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Establish baseline blood-borne virus status after the exposure; arrange via GP/pathology within 48 hours.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Repeat/follow-up blood-borne virus serology at the seroconversion window (per guideline follow-up schedule)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Detect any seroconversion at the appropriate interval after a documented exposure.
Not indicated for a clean, uninfected minor puncture; reserve for later signs of wound infection.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Immediate HIV viral load / broad panic testing at presentation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Not clinically useful acutely and would reinforce health-anxiety; baseline serology plus scheduled follow-up is the correct approach.
